sections.a_eyebrow: Heavy Motion · WebGL · Shaders · Advanced JS · 3D Animation
sections.a_description: Fragment shaders painting pixels, noise functions generating organic distortions, and the raw mathematical beauty of GLSL running in real-time — WebGL Shader Effects design is where code becomes visual art. The pure black backgrounds serve as canvases for programs that manipulate every pixel, while hot magenta accents and monospace typography connect the visual output to the code that generates it.

This shader-driven style powers creative coding experiments, digital art portfolios, music visualizers, and immersive brand experiences. You'll see it on Shadertoy-inspired galleries, demo scene showcases, generative art platforms, and experimental interactive installations — anywhere that GLSL fragment and vertex shaders transform the browser into a real-time graphics processing canvas.

a_image_cover.alt: WebGL Shader Effects style use case illustration
WebGL Shader Effects style use case illustration
WebGL Shader Effects style use case illustration

sections.a_description: The experimental, code-art aesthetic of WebGL shader websites — raw GLSL-powered visuals, noise-driven distortions, and fragment shader artistry rendered directly in the browser. Background: pure black (#000000) — the void that shader programs paint upon. The .inner panel has a dark translucent background (rgba(255,255,255,0.03)) with a thin border (1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.08)) — barely visible UI hovering over shader-generated visuals. Colors: hot magenta (#ec4899) as the primary accent — vibrant and electric, the kind of color that emerges from noise functions and UV manipulations. Light gray (#d1d5db) for headings, muted gray (#9ca3af) for body text. The aesthetic is deeply technical — code as art, math as beauty. Set in JetBrains Mono — because shader code IS the medium, and the monospace font connects the visual output to the GLSL source that created it. Heading at weight 700, 24px, line-height 1.3, color #d1d5db. Eyebrow at weight 500, 11px, uppercase, 3px letter-spacing, color #ec4899. Body at weight 400, 13px, line-height 1.7, color #9ca3af. Buttons: primary — magenta (#ec4899), white text, 0 border-radius. Secondary: transparent, magenta border. Alternatives: Fira Code (ligature monospace), IBM Plex Mono (clean monospace).
